chapters.title: Chapter 1 Classical Formation
chapters.description: In the classical formation after ...Nc6, d6, a6 and Nf6, Black threatens to halt White's momentum with ...Bg4. White can choose between 8. Bf4 and the adventurous, Evans-style b4.

chapters.title: Chapter 2 Scheveningen Formation
chapters.description: In Chapters 2 and 3, Black establishes the modest e6/d6 central pawn duo that resembles the Scheveningen structure, one of Black's most solid and flexible defensive systems in the Open Sicilian.

chapters.title: Chapter 3 Scheveningen Formation II
chapters.description: In Chapters 2 and 3, Black establishes the modest e6/d6 central pawn duo that resembles the Scheveningen structure, one of Black's most solid and flexible defensive systems in the Open Sicilian.

chapters.title: Chapter 4 Siberian Variation
chapters.description: The Siberian Variation, where Black plays ...e6, Nc6 and Qc7, takes its name from the following trap: 8. Qe2?! Ng4 9. h3? Nd4. Instead, White should opt for 8. Nb5.

chapters.title: Chapter 5 Dragon Formation
chapters.description: Black intends to enter Dragon territory with kingside fianchetto. Standard development won't suffice for the gambiteer. White should either pressure f7 with Qb3 to provoke ...e6; or in other lines play 7. e5 to discourage ...d6.

chapters.title: Chapter 6 Paulsen Formation
chapters.description: After ...Nge7, Black plans to refute the gambit via ...Ng6, Be7 and O-O. The gambiteer halts Black's development with Bg5, provoking ...f6/h6.

chapters.title: Chapter 7 Kan Formation
chapters.description: Instead of committing the knight to c6, Black opts for an early queenside expansion with ...a6, b5 and Bb7. In the main line, White strikes with 9. Ng5, immediately targeting the weakened e6-square.

chapters.title: Chapter 8 Pin Defense
chapters.description: In this chapter, Black pins White's knight with ...Bb4. While the premature 5...Bb4 is met by Qd4, the main line sees White respond with Qc2 and Rd1.

chapters.title: Chapter 9 Morphy Defense
chapters.description: Only after ...Bc5 does Black aim for ...Nge7 and O-O, rendering Bg5 less effective. White responds with e5, intending Ne4 to follow.

chapters.title: Chapter 10 Finegold Defense
chapters.description: In this chapter, Black defers ...Nc6, retaining the option of ...Nbd7. The mainline continues 8... Be7 (the Finegold Defense), while 8... b5 leads to the Chicago Defense, typically followed by the ...Ra7 rook lift.

chapters.title: Chapter 11 Morra Declined Miscellany
chapters.description: Compare Smith-Morra declined structures and early deviations. Study how White deals with miscellaneous Black responses.

chapters.title: Chapter 12 Push Variation
chapters.description: The "Maroczy Bind" is set up against 3...d3. Notice White's move order: prophylactic h3 preventing ...Bg4/Ng4, and Nc3 after ...Nf6, avoiding ...Bxc3.
